titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Douglas ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Douglas ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 2420. Following obtained informally from Burrows today regarding Palestine:

list.item: As British Delegation UN will explain to USUN today Foreign Office feels that while it agrees that other questions of interpretation should be left to Bernadotte, Foreign Office feels regarding paragraphs three and four of SC resolution Bernadotte should have guidance because HMG has already committed itself to Arabs regarding its interpretation, If Bernadotte should take contrary view, Arabs would claim that they had been tricked. Possible alternative, though less satisfactory, line would be Bernadotte ruling that men of military age should not be allowed enter Palestine until there are adequate safeguards to insure that these men are not used for military service (Department’s 2015 May 31 and 2026 June 1The latter was a repeat of telegram 350 to New York, p. 1086.).

Foreign Office understands Bernadotte created favorable impression
Amman where he pleased Arabs by showing willingness make decisions and accept responsibility for implementation SC resolution.

div.p: Repeated Baghdad 60, Cairo 76, Beirut 26, Damascus 30, Jidda 72. Department please repeat Palestine.
